mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month through valuation compression.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower when FEDFUNDS rises (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering negative risk sentiment and downward S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances when UNRATE crosses the threshold, or signal accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between real GDP growth and real personal consumption expenditures indicates structural demand weakness, causing institutional investors to reduce equity allocations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in quarterly return regressions over a 5-year sample, or predictive power vanishes after controlling for monetary policy.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines during monetary tightening cycles reflect constrained supply capacity, forcing firms to cut capital expenditures and earnings guidance, driving negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods show no significant correlation with 20-day forward returns (t-stat < 1.5), or the signal reverses direction in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation runs above 3% annually while the federal funds rate remains below 2%, real rates turn deeply negative, fueling speculative equity buying and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive returns fail to materialize in >60% of triggered months, or the joint effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in multivariate regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate consumer spending capacity, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS growth falls below 0.02, or quarterly returns are negative in >50% of expansion months across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in housing starts signals an imminent housing market correction, which rapidly transmits to financial sector earnings and consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in >50% of instances following a >10% monthly housing starts drop, or the threshold effect disappears after 2020.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and rising unemployment confirm a broad economic contraction, triggering flight-to-safety flows that suppress S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns with >65% accuracy over a 10-year out-of-sample test, or interaction coefficient is statistically insignificant.
